user_query:
Build a market intelligence dossier for my small portfolio. Read input/portfolio.json to get my holdings and settings. Then:

1) Generate a portfolio feed using the holdings (use equal weights if any are missing). Save the unedited raw response to output/raw/portfolio.txt.
2) Get a sector overview for the number of days specified in the file and save it to output/raw/sectors_{sector_days}.txt.
3) For each ticker, fetch a basic overview and a trading signal; save each unedited raw response to output/raw/{TICKER}_ticker.txt and output/raw/{TICKER}_score.txt (use uppercase ticker symbols in filenames).
4) Pull the latest briefs for the category and limit provided in the file and save the unedited raw response to output/raw/news_{news_category}.txt.

Then compile an Obsidian-ready summary at output/report.md with:
- YAML frontmatter including title: "Portfolio Dossier", today’s date (YYYY-MM-DD), and tags: [portfolio, market-intel].
- Sections in this order: "## Portfolio", "## Sectors", "## News", "## Tickers".
- In each section, embed the matching raw file using Obsidian embed syntax:
  - Portfolio: ![[raw/portfolio.txt]]
  - Sectors: ![[raw/sectors_{sector_days}.txt]]
  - News: ![[raw/news_{news_category}.txt]]
- Under "## Tickers", list each ticker as a wikilink to a note named exactly after the ticker, e.g., [[NVDA]].

Also create per-ticker notes under output/notes/ (one per ticker, e.g., output/notes/NVDA.md). Each note must:
- Include YAML frontmatter with title: "{TICKER} Note" and tags: [ticker].
- Start with a collapsible callout acknowledging the data source (keep the provider’s attribution footer intact in all included text).
- Embed both raw files for that ticker using relative paths from the note folder:
  - ![[../raw/{TICKER}_ticker.txt]]
  - ![[../raw/{TICKER}_score.txt]]

Do not alter the returned text; preserve the provider’s footer and URLs. Use uppercase tickers when naming files and links. Write only to output/.
